title: Reserve RBA

tabs.problem: Customers needed a way to increase their 30-day card limits without waiting on manual underwriting. The existing flow was slow and didn't leverage existing Treasury balances.
When customers deposit a balance into their Reserve RBA, they expect their 30-day card limit to go up by the deposited amount.

problem: Customers needed a way to increase their 30-day card limits without waiting on manual underwriting. The existing flow was slow and didn't leverage existing Treasury balances.
projectDescription: Led design for self-serve Reserve account activation, allowing customers to instantly increase 30-day card limits 1:1 by depositing funds while earning 2% yield

impact.title: Projected Treasury impact
impact.description: Projected to add $180M in Treasury AUM annually and $10M in contribution profit while reducing CLIP volume by 30%.

title: Treasury dashboard v1
projectDescription: First version of the Treasury dashboard giving finance teams a single view of cash positions, yield, and linked accounts.
problem: Finance teams were juggling multiple tools to see treasury balances and yield; we needed one place to see it all.

impact.title: Unified view
impact.description: Replaced multiple spreadsheets and bank portals with one dashboard.

title: Cash flow visibility
projectDescription: Designed flows and UI for forecasting and visualizing cash flow so teams could plan ahead and avoid shortfalls.
problem: Customers couldn't easily see projected cash flow or understand when balances might run low.

title: Bank connection flows
projectDescription: End-to-end design for connecting external bank accounts to Treasury: auth, account selection, and verification.
problem: Linking bank accounts had to feel secure and simple so customers could fund Treasury without drop-off.

title: Spend limits UI
projectDescription: UI for setting and managing card and category spend limits so admins could enforce policy without blocking day-to-day use.
problem: Admins needed granular control over who could spend how much, without creating friction for employees.

impact.title: Policy compliance
impact.description: Clear limits reduced out-of-policy spend and support tickets.

title: Approval workflows
projectDescription: Designed approval chains for card requests and spend so companies could enforce multi-step sign-off before cards were issued.
problem: Finance wanted requests to route through managers and approvers before going live.

title: Reporting exports
projectDescription: Reporting and export experience so admins could pull spend data by category, card, or time range for reconciliation and audits.
problem: Teams needed to export clean data for accounting and compliance without manual cleanup.

title: Onboarding flow
projectDescription: First-run experience for developers signing up for Kurtosis: install, auth, and first environment so they could run a tutorial in minutes.
problem: Dev tools lose users at setup; we needed a minimal path from signup to "I ran something."

title: Docs navigation
projectDescription: Information architecture and navigation for Kurtosis docs so developers could find concepts, API reference, and tutorials quickly.
problem: Docs were growing; we needed a structure that scaled without hiding key content.

title: CLI feedback patterns
projectDescription: Consistent patterns for progress, success, and errors in the Kurtosis CLI so output was scannable and actionable.
problem: CLI output was inconsistent; users couldn't tell if a command was still running or had failed.

title: Component variants
projectDescription: Design and documentation for component variants in the design system so designers could switch states and props without leaving the canvas.
problem: Components had many states; we needed a clear way to browse and apply variants.

title: Design tokens
projectDescription: Token structure and naming for color, spacing, and typography so design and code stayed in sync.
problem: Design and eng were drifting on values; tokens gave a single source of truth.

title: Plugin API docs
projectDescription: Documentation and examples for the Plugin API so internal and external developers could build Figma plugins with less back-and-forth.
problem: Plugin adoption was blocked by unclear docs and missing examples.

title: Ad format picker
projectDescription: UI for choosing ad formats (image, video, carousel) so advertisers could match format to campaign goals without confusion.
problem: Advertisers didn't know which format to pick; we needed guidance and clear tradeoffs.

title: Audience targeting UI
projectDescription: Targeting controls for audience selection so advertisers could define who saw their ads while staying within policy.
problem: Targeting was powerful but dense; we had to simplify without hiding important options.

title: Performance tables
projectDescription: Tables and filters for ad performance so advertisers could compare campaigns and optimize spend.
problem: Performance data was overwhelming; we needed sortable, filterable tables with clear metrics.

title: Member directory
projectDescription: Member directory so the community could find and connect with other members by role, location, or interest.
problem: Members wanted to network but had no structured way to discover each other.

title: Event signup
projectDescription: Event signup and confirmation flow so members could register for workshops and events and get reminders.
problem: Signup had to be simple and reliable so attendance stayed high.

title: Resource library
projectDescription: Resource library for career and learning materials so members could find guides, links, and downloads in one place.
problem: Resources were scattered; we needed categories and search so content was findable.

title: Website refresh
projectDescription: Full refresh of the WICC website so the org had a clear, up-to-date presence for recruiting and events.
problem: The old site was outdated and didn't reflect the current mission or events.

title: Event pages
projectDescription: Templates and layout for event pages so each event had a consistent place for details, signup, and updates.
problem: Events were announced in many places; we needed one canonical page per event.

title: Newsletter layout
projectDescription: Newsletter layout and structure so WICC could send regular updates that were easy to scan and link back to the site.
problem: Newsletters were plain text; we wanted a recognizable, readable format that matched the brand.

title: Workshop materials
projectDescription: Templates and slide decks for CuXD workshops so facilitators could run consistent, well-structured sessions.
problem: Every workshop was built from scratch; we needed reusable materials that still felt fresh.

title: Project showcase site
projectDescription: Showcase site for club projects so members could share work and visitors could see what CuXD did.
problem: Projects lived in slides and Figma; we wanted one place to highlight outcomes.

title: Design crit format
projectDescription: Structured format for design critiques so feedback was constructive and time-boxed.
problem: Crits could run long or feel vague; we needed a repeatable format that helped everyone participate.

title: App navigation
projectDescription: Primary navigation for the campus app so students could jump between academics, events, and services without getting lost.
problem: The app had many sections; we needed a nav that scaled and stayed familiar.

title: Notification settings
projectDescription: Notification preferences so users could choose what they got (push, email, in-app) and reduce noise.
problem: Users were overwhelmed by alerts; we needed granular control without complexity.

title: Profile flows
projectDescription: Profile and account flows so students could update info, photo, and preferences in one place.
problem: Profile was scattered across the app; we consolidated into a single, clear flow.

title: Scheduling UI
projectDescription: Scheduling interface for staff and patients so appointments could be booked and managed without double-booking or confusion.
problem: Scheduling had to respect clinical constraints and be easy for both staff and patients.

title: Patient intake forms
projectDescription: Digital intake forms so patients could complete history and consent before visits, reducing wait and errors.
problem: Paper intake was slow and led to missing or illegible data; digital had to be accessible and compliant.

title: Dashboard widgets
projectDescription: Dashboard widgets for internal tools so staff could see key metrics and tasks at a glance.
problem: Staff had to open multiple tools to get a picture of the day; we needed a single dashboard that was configurable.
